question: Compare the NPM schematic with secondary equipment (Figure 1.2-3, Ch.01) with the SG assembly detail (Figure 5.4-1, Ch.05).

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and ECCS valves, and associated piping and systems. It details the integration of these components within the RPV, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary sides, and references where further design specifics can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch01_0017.title: Facility Description
nuscale_ch01_0017.summary: The Facility Description section outlines the NuScale plant’s core and cooling system operations. It explains how heat generated in the reactor core is transferred via natural circulation to steam generators, producing superheated steam that drives a turbine and generates electricity. The section also notes that site-specific details exist for the cooling water system and cooling towers, with further information provided elsewhere in the report.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains two steam generators (SGs) integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The steam generator system (SGS) includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs. The SG tubes and associated welds are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, ensuring separation between the primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch01_0016.title: Principal Site Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0016.summary: This section provides a general overview of the NuScale plant design, outlining key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and supplement it with site-specific information, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI) that are outside the certified scope. Security-related details are specially marked and withheld in accordance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for both electrical/I&C and piping/instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be provided by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0057.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - NuFuel HTP2™ Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0057.summary: This section describes the critical heat flux (CHF) testing conducted for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which uses AREVA HMP™/HTP™ spacer grid technology. The testing aimed to gather CHF data to develop and validate NuScale's CHF correlations, as well as to measure bundle subchannel exit temperatures and pressure drop characteristics under various hydraulic conditions. The tests were performed using a 5x5 electrically-heated simulated fuel bundle at the AREVA Karlstein facility in Germany.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using thermal hydraulic software and hand verification account for uncertainties and ensure the natural circulation design is effective, with low pressure losses and robust performance across design conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0056.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - Preliminary Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0056.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation tests conducted to support the safety analysis of the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on unique design features lacking prior data or operational experience. Specifically, it describes two critical heat flux (CHF) test programs for the NuScale fuel design—one for the preliminary design (to support code development and safety analysis) and another for the final design—with the preliminary tests providing essential data for developing the NSP2 CHF correlation.

nuscale_ch01_0774.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) Last two sentences of the last paragraph of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Plant-Specific Technical Specifications for Primary and Secondary Coolant System Iodine Activity
nuscale_ch01_0774.summary: The section explains that while the guidance on technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity is applicable to the NuScale design, the radiological acceptance criteria are instead governed by SRP Section 15.0.3, which supersedes the original criteria. Additionally, certain sections and criteria are noted as not applicable to the NuScale design, particularly those relevant only to BWRs or superseded by other regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings are included in the report to visually clarify design information, with legends provided for symbols used in these diagrams. It lists the types of diagrams available, such as functional and one-line diagrams for electrical and I&C systems, as well as piping and instrumentation diagrams, and notes that site-specific diagrams will be provided by applicants referencing the NuScale Power Plant design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature measurement devices, flow meters, level sensors, and other auxiliary instrumentation components, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It details the permissions granted to the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for internal use and public disclosure, emphasizing that proprietary information is protected and copying is limited without NuScale Power LLC's written consent.

nuscale_ch01_0108.title: 8.37 ALARA Levels for Effluents from Materials Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0108.summary: This section provides a legend for electrical symbols used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, specifically for single-line diagrams. It details the meanings of various symbols and annotations related to transformers, circuit breakers, batteries, switches, and other electrical components, enabling accurate interpretation of the facility’s electrical drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified plant portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. The section also notes that COL applicants must address all COL information items and provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0116.title: Figure 1.7-3e: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0116.summary: This section provides a comprehensive list of legends and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs), detailing the symbols for various analysis functions, measurement types, and instrumentation identifiers. It serves as a reference to interpret the diagrams by defining acronyms for sensors, analyzers, and measurement methods related to process variables such as radiation, flow, chemical composition, and physical properties.

nuscale_ch01_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch01_0000.summary: This preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifying that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document as needed for regulatory purposes. It also notes that proprietary information is protected, and any copies made must include the appropriate copyright and proprietary notices.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed design information about the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B offers a general facility and process overview that references the location and function of these components, thus Section A specifies the technical details for the general descriptions found in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the NPM schematic and its major subsystems, including the steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the steam generators as a component detailed in the schematic.

Section A provides detailed design information about the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, which are generally described as part of the overall facility and its systems in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the plant layout and the integration of the reactor core within the RPV, while Section B specifies the detailed components inside the RPV, including the steam generators, thus Section B provides detailed specifications for the general description in Section A.

Section A provides specific details about the Site Cooling Water System and reactor core assembly, which are components generally described in Section B's overall summary of plant systems including cooling water and auxiliary systems.
Section A provides detailed design information about the steam generators within the NPM, which are generally described as part of the overall facility and its systems in Section B.

Section A provides schematic diagrams that visually represent the arrangement and connections of secondary equipment, which are further detailed in Section B's description of the steam generator (SG) assembly and its integration within the reactor coolant system.

Section A provides schematic diagrams that visually represent the secondary equipment and system layout, which includes components described in Section B's facility description, thereby offering detailed visual context for the general plant and system overview in Section B.

Section A provides schematic diagrams that include the secondary equipment layout for the NPM, while Section B details the specific design and assembly of the steam generators, which are components depicted in Section A's schematics.

Section A provides critical heat flux testing data and correlations for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which is a key component within the reactor core described in Section B's schematic of the reactor coolant system and its subsystems, including the steam generators.

Section A provides test data and correlations for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design's critical heat flux, which is a key input for understanding the thermal performance of the reactor core described in Section B's facility overview.

Section A provides critical heat flux testing data for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which is directly relevant to the thermal-hydraulic performance and safety analysis of the steam generators described in Section B, as both sections address components and phenomena within the integrated NuScale Power Module (NPM) schematic.

Section A references the SGs and directs the reader to Section 5.4 for further description, which is provided in Section B, thus connecting the general system evaluation to the detailed component design.

Section A references the steam generator (SG) and its role in decay heat removal, while Section B provides a schematic overview of the reactor and its connections, including the hot leg riser, thus both sections are connected by their descriptions of the physical arrangement and function of secondary equipment in the NPM schematic.

Section A references Section B for further details about the steam generators (SGs), indicating that the general system evaluation in Section A is supplemented by the specific component and subsystem design information in Section B.

Section A describes verification and confirmation tests for unique design features that support the safety analysis, while Section B details the components and subsystems of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators whose design features are subject to the testing described in Section A.

Section A describes testing programs that generate data to support the safety analysis of design features, while Section B provides a general description of the facility and its systems, with both sections connected through the need for test data (from A) to validate or support the design descriptions and operational claims made in B.

Section A describes testing programs that generate data to support the safety analysis of unique design features, which include components like the steam generators detailed in Section B, thereby providing evidence that supports the design claims made in Section B.

Section A references regulatory requirements for primary and secondary coolant system iodine activity, which are directly related to the design and configuration of the reactor coolant system and steam generators described in Section B.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for iodine activity in primary and secondary coolant systems, which are directly influenced by the design and operation of secondary equipment such as the steam generator detailed in Section B's facility description and schematics.

Section A references regulatory requirements for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity in the context of steam generator tube failure, while Section B describes the physical design and components of the steam generators, thus Section B provides the detailed design context necessary to evaluate compliance with the requirements discussed in Section A.

Section A provides schematic and diagrammatic details of system components, including the steam generator (SG), while Section B discusses critical heat flux testing data and correlations that are directly relevant to the SG's fuel design and operational safety, thus Section A's visual and design information supports the technical context for Section B's testing and validation.

Section A provides schematic diagrams of the NPM and secondary equipment, which visually represent the system components and their interconnections that are further detailed and evaluated in Section B's discussion of the SG assembly and its role in system performance.

Section A provides schematic diagrams and visual clarifications of system components, which are referenced in Section B as the basis for verification and confirmation tests supporting the safety analysis.

Section A provides schematic and detailed drawings of the NPM and secondary equipment, which visually clarify the design and configuration relevant to the SG assembly details shown in Section B, thereby supporting the evaluation of compliance with radiological technical specifications for coolant system iodine activity.

Section A provides critical heat flux data and correlations for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which are necessary inputs for the system evaluation in Section B that establishes steady state parameters and optimizes performance of the NuScale Power Plant, including the steam generator (SG) operation.

Section A describes the critical heat flux testing for the updated NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which builds upon and augments the preliminary fuel design testing described in Section B by generating new data and validating previous correlations.

Section A provides critical heat flux testing data for the NuFuel HTP2™ fuel design, which is relevant for understanding fuel performance and safety margins in the NPM schematic (Figure 1.2-3), while Section B discusses regulatory requirements for coolant system iodine activity in the context of steam generator tube failure, connecting both sections through their implications for radiological safety and system design as depicted in the referenced figures.

Section A references the detailed description of the steam generators (SGs) in Section 5.4, while Section B outlines testing programs that provide data to support the safety analyses of unique design features such as the SGs, thus the testing in B supports the system evaluation and design optimization discussed in A.

Section A describes the operational parameters and performance criteria for the steam generators (SGs), while Section B references regulatory requirements for iodine activity in the primary and secondary coolant systems, which are directly influenced by the SG design and operation described in Section A.

Section A describes testing programs that generate data supporting safety analyses, which are necessary to demonstrate conformance with the regulatory requirements for radiological consequences of steam generator tube failure discussed in Section B.

Section A provides schematic drawings, including the NPM schematic with secondary equipment, which visually represent and clarify the arrangement and interconnections of components such as the steam generators that are described in detail in Section B's SG assembly detail.

Section A provides schematic drawings that visually clarify the design and arrangement of secondary equipment, which includes components described in Section B's facility description such as the site cooling water system and its integration with the reactor core and support assemblies.

Section A provides general schematic drawings of the NPM and its secondary equipment, which include simplified representations of components like the steam generators, while Section B offers detailed assembly information specifically about the steam generators depicted in Section A's schematic.

Section A provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the schematic symbols used in Section B's detailed reactor coolant system and steam generator assembly drawings, enabling accurate understanding of the depicted components.

Section A provides the diagram legends necessary to interpret the schematic representations of secondary equipment in Section B's facility description, thereby enabling accurate understanding of the equipment and process flows discussed.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret the piping and instrumentation diagrams that include secondary equipment in the NPM schematic referenced in Section B's description of the steam generator assembly.

Section A provides the copyright and publication context for the NuScale Design Certification Application, of which Section B is a technical excerpt detailing the reactor coolant system component and subsystem design.

Section B provides a general facility and process overview that contextualizes the NPM schematic referenced in Section A, connecting the schematic's depiction of secondary equipment to the broader plant system described in Section B.

Section A provides the general context and copyright information for Chapter Five, which contains the detailed design information about the reactor coolant system and its components, including the steam generators described in Section B.

Section A provides the schematic symbols and legend necessary to interpret the NPM and secondary equipment schematic (Figure 1.2-3), which is required to understand the detailed SG assembly shown in Section B's Figure 5.4-1, thus Section A is a prerequisite for comprehending the technical details in Section B.

Section A provides the schematic symbols and diagram legends necessary to interpret the electrical and secondary equipment layouts referenced in Section B's general plant and system descriptions, enabling a detailed understanding of the equipment connections and functions described in the facility overview.

Section A provides the schematic symbols and legend necessary to interpret the electrical and secondary equipment connections shown in Figure 1.2-3, which are relevant for understanding the integration and interface of secondary-side equipment with the steam generator assembly detailed in Section B's Figure 5.4-1.

Section A defines the interface boundaries and identifies which components, such as the steam generators, are part of the certified design, while Section B provides detailed design information about those components, specifically the steam generators within the reactor coolant system.

Section A outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and site-specific design, while Section B provides a general description of the facility and identifies which systems and components are site-specific, thus Section B specifies the context and details that Section A references in defining design interfaces.

Section A defines the interface boundaries and identifies which components, such as the steam generators, are included in the certified design, while Section B provides detailed design information about the steam generators themselves, connecting the general interface description to specific component details.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation and component identifiers (such as 'SG' for strain gauge) used in schematics like Figure 1.2-3, which are necessary to interpret the secondary equipment layout and understand the specific components referenced in the SG assembly detail described in Section B.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation and function identifiers used in diagrams like the NPM schematic referenced in Section B's facility description, enabling interpretation of the schematic's symbols in the context of the plant's systems.

Section A provides the legend for instrumentation and function identifiers (such as 'SG' for strain gauge) used in schematic diagrams like Figure 1.2-3, which are necessary to interpret the secondary equipment layout and understand the instrumentation details referenced in Section B's discussion of the steam generator assembly.

Section A provides the general introduction and context for the NuScale plant, while Section B gives detailed design information about the reactor coolant system and its components, including the steam generators referenced in the schematic from Section A.

Section A introduces the NuScale plant and references the schematic in Chapter 1, while Section B provides a detailed facility description that elaborates on the systems depicted in the schematic, specifically connecting the overview in Section A to the process details in Section B.

Section A provides a general schematic overview of the NPM and its secondary equipment, while Section B gives detailed assembly information about the steam generators that are depicted in the schematic, thus Section B specifies the technical details for the general components shown in Section A.
Section A provides diagram legends that are used to interpret schematic figures such as those referenced in Section B's chapter, thus enabling understanding of the SG assembly detail in Chapter Five.

Both Section A and Section B provide diagram legends for interpreting different types of schematics (instrumentation/piping vs. electrical), which are necessary for understanding and comparing the NPM schematic and SG assembly detail as required by the analysis question.

Section A provides diagram legends used to interpret schematic figures like those referenced in Section B, which discusses interface requirements and identifies figures representing system boundaries.

Section A provides the legend for auxiliary and accessory device symbols used in piping and instrumentation diagrams, while Section B provides the legend for function identifiers used in the same diagrams, meaning both sections specify different but complementary aspects necessary to interpret the same schematic figures.

Section A provides diagram legends used in the NuScale FSAR figures, which are referenced in Section B's general description of the plant documentation, enabling interpretation of schematic figures like those compared in the analysis question.

Section A provides the copyright and document control information for Chapter Five, which contains detailed design information such as the SG assembly detail, while Section B describes how the certified design (including schematics like Figure 1.2-3) interfaces with site-specific elements, connecting the figures referenced in the analysis question through their roles in defining design scope and boundaries.

Section A is a preface providing copyright and usage information for Chapter Five of the NuScale Design Certification Application, while Section B provides diagram legends for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams referenced in the application; thus, Section B supports the understanding and use of figures and schematics that may be discussed or included in Section A's referenced chapters.

Section A provides detailed electrical schematic symbols and legend information that are used to interpret the facility schematics referenced in Section B, which discusses the interface requirements and identifies figures (such as Figure 1.2-1 and 1.2-2) that represent the overall facility and its boundaries.

Section A provides the legend for electrical symbols used in single-line diagrams, while Section B provides the legend for instrumentation and analysis function identifiers used in piping and instrumentation diagrams; both sections specify the meaning of symbols used in different types of technical schematics, enabling accurate interpretation of Figures 1.2-3 and 5.4-1.
Section A provides specific schematic and symbol details used in the NuScale FSAR figures, which are referenced in Section B's general introduction to the plant design documentation.

Section A references the figures and boundaries of certified design components, while Section B provides the legend necessary to interpret the symbols used in such figures, connecting them by enabling understanding of schematic diagrams mentioned in Section A.

Section A references Section B by indicating that Section 1.2 (from Chapter One, as introduced in Section B) identifies the structures, systems, and components included in the certified design, which is foundational for understanding the interface requirements discussed in Section A.

Section A provides the legend and symbol definitions necessary to interpret diagrams such as those referenced in Section B's introductory and copyright context, enabling accurate understanding of schematic figures like Figure 1.2-3.
